expectations.text: All inline comments land on valid diff positions (no 422 errors)
expectations.evidence: Without the skill, naive Claude would not know to verify hunk ranges before posting. It would attempt to post at the line number from the file (e.g., line 229 in cron-hydra.sh) without converting to a diff position. The GitHub API would return 422 errors: 'pull_request_review_thread.position is not part of the diff' — no inline comments would land.

expectations.text: Comment positions derived from hunk headers (@@ -old,len +new,len @@), not guessed
expectations.evidence: Without the skill's hunk-parsing protocol, naive Claude would use the file's line number directly as the 'position' parameter. File line numbers != diff positions. The diff position is the 1-indexed offset from the first @@ hunk header, counting all diff lines (context, added, removed).

expectations.text: Finds that the hardcoded-path finding (line 229 in cron-hydra.sh) is in the diff
expectations.evidence: Without explicitly checking the diff file, naive Claude might find the hardcoded path by reading the file directly (not the diff). Reading the file at HEAD would show it, but naive Claude cannot determine if that line is part of the current PR diff or was pre-existing — it might post a comment on a pre-existing line, which is not in the diff.

expectations.text: Does not attempt to post a comment on a file not in the diff
expectations.evidence: Without enumerating the PR's changed files first, naive Claude might try to post comments on files it reads during analysis (e.g., reading images/builder/CLAUDE.md for context) even if those files are not part of the PR diff.

notes: Line number accuracy is entirely dependent on the skill's hunk-range verification protocol. Without it, every inline comment attempt would fail with 422 errors — naive Claude would produce no inline comments at all on a large PR.
